rows.requirement: Sustained concurrent ingestion+query load run over an extended (documented, bounded) duration.
rows.covered_by: TestSoak: writer pool (CommitVersion) + query pool (SnapshotRead) run concurrently against shared fileID pool until a shared deadline, default duration overridable via SOAK_DURATION.
rows.method: real -race execution, output captured in self-consistency.json

rows.covered_by: go test itself fails/panics visibly if any goroutine panics uncaught; test asserts zero errors returned from every CommitVersion/SnapshotRead call.
rows.method: real execution; non-zero exit / panic trace would be captured verbatim

rows.covered_by: runtime.NumGoroutine() snapshot before pool start vs. after wg.Wait(), asserted within a documented generous slack.
rows.method: real execution; numbers logged and asserted

rows.covered_by: runtime.ReadMemStats HeapAlloc snapshot before vs. after (each preceded by runtime.GC()), asserted against a documented generous ceiling multiple.
rows.method: real execution; numbers logged and asserted

rows.covered_by: Every CommitVersion/SnapshotRead call's error is checked; SnapshotRead's returned bytes are checked non-empty (a correctness violation in the MVCC read path would surface as an error or empty read here, consistent with query_latency_test.go's precedent).
Plan step 5: if real run surfaces a failure, diagnose + fix + re-run to green before commit.
rows.method: conditional; see self-consistency.json for whether this was triggered

rows.covered_by: TestSoak is the exact name; run with -timeout sized to comfortably exceed SOAK_DURATION.

rows.requirement: No regression to existing engine/loadtest tests (6.3.1-6.3.4).
rows.covered_by: Full package test run (go test ./engine/loadtest/... -race, no -run filter, short timeout excluded per testing.Short semantics as needed).
